Title: CfAPX, a cytosolic ascorbate peroxidase gene from Cryptomeria fortunei, confers tolerance to abiotic stress in transgenic Arabidopsis

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: CfAPX encodes a 249-amino-acid, 27.22 kDa hydrophilic enzyme lacking transmembrane regions; it contains conserved heme-, substrate- and K+-binding sites characteristic of plant peroxidases. CfAPX shows no transcriptional self-activation activity in yeast. This is the first APX homolog functionally characterized from C. fortunei.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. CfAPX cDNA from Cryptomeria fortunei encodes a 249-aa, 27.22 kDa hydrophilic ascorbate peroxidase belonging to the cytosolic APX (cAPX) subgroup and plant peroxidase family, with conserved heme-, substrate- and K+-binding sites.
2. Subcellular localization shows CfAPX is cytoplasmic and lacks transcriptional self-activation activity in yeast.
3. CfAPX is constitutively expressed in C. fortunei with highest levels in cones and is strongly induced by salt, drought, heat and cold stresses in both C. fortunei and CfAPX-overexpressing Arabidopsis.
4. Heterologous expression of CfAPX in yeast and overexpression in Arabidopsis significantly enhance tolerance to salt, drought, heat and freezing by increasing antioxidant enzyme activities and AsA content, reducing H2O2 and MDA accumulation, and maintaining chlorophyll content, PSII efficiency and survival under stress.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: C. fortunei transcriptome (high-throughput sequencing) used to build a local BLAST database from which CfAPX transcript CL8464.Contig3_All was identified.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CfAPX, a cytosolic ascorbate peroxidase from C. fortunei, confers enhanced tolerance to salt, drought, heat and freezing stress by increasing antioxidant capacity, reducing ROS and membrane lipid peroxidation, and maintaining chlorophyll content and PSII photochemical efficiency.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: CfAPX overexpression increases APX activity and AsA content, enhancing the AsA-dependent reduction of H2O2 and lowering H2O2 and MDA accumulation; this is accompanied by higher activities of other antioxidant enzymes (SOD, POD, CAT) and better preservation of chlorophyll and PSII photochemistry, thereby mitigating oxidative damage under abiotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Antioxidant defense and ROS-scavenging pathway via the ascorbate–glutathione cycle during abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ascorbate–glutathione (AsA–GSH–NADPH) cycle for H2O2 scavenging in ROS metabolism
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Ascorbate peroxidase (APX), member of the plant peroxidase (POD) family, containing conserved heme-binding, substrate-binding and K+-binding sites.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm, as shown by 35S::CfAPX-GFP fusion protein localized to the cytoplasm in Nicotiana benthamiana leaf epidermal cells.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In C. fortunei, CfAPX is constitutively expressed with highest transcript levels in cones, followed by young needles, and much lower levels (about 20–25% of cones) in stems, seeds and roots. Under salt (200 mM NaCl), drought (10% PEG-6000), heat (42°C) and cold (4°C) treatments, CfAPX expression in C. fortunei is induced, typically increasing to 3.4–3.6-fold of control at 12 h for salt and drought, peaking early (about 306% of control at 2 h) under heat then decreasing, and reaching about 1.6-fold at 24 h under cold. In Arabidopsis, CfAPX expression in 35S::CfAPX plants is much higher than in WT and further increases transiently under salt, drought, heat and cold, peaking at 2.5–4.7-fold the WT level at specific time points.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress (up to 200 mM NaCl in media or solution), osmotic/drought stress (mannitol up to 400 mM; 10% PEG-6000), heat stress (40°C for Arabidopsis, 42°C for C. fortunei), cold stress (4°C) and freezing stress (-6°C) applied to seedlings and cuttings for defined durations.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance and ROS scavenging in trees; functional characterization of ascorbate peroxidase in C. fortunei.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salt stress tolerance, drought/osmotic stress tolerance, heat stress tolerance, cold/freezing stress tolerance) and oxidative stress response.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under salt and drought treatments, chlorophyll a, b and a+b and PSII parameters (Fv/Fm, qP, YII) in CfAPX transgenic Arabidopsis were approximately 2.17–13.64% higher than in WT; APX, SOD, POD and CAT activities and AsA content were about 5.70–54.55% higher; H2O2 and MDA contents were reduced by about 3.66–33.72% and 2.20–5.29%, respectively. Under freezing stress, transgenic plants had a survival rate of about 64.67%, significantly higher than WT, and lower electrolyte leakage. In C. fortunei, CfAPX expression under salt and drought reached about 3.4–3.6-fold that of control at peak, increased about 306% under heat at 2 h, and about 1.62-fold under cold at 24 h. In Arabidopsis, CfAPX expression in transgenics under stress peaked at ~2.54–4.69-fold WT levels depending on treatment.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Transgenic Arabidopsis overexpressing CfAPX shows higher seed germination and longer roots than WT under 200 mM NaCl or 400 mM mannitol; more leaves and higher survival after prolonged salt, drought, heat or freezing treatments; reduced electrolyte leakage under freezing; and better recovery growth after stress.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Two-year-old #57 C. fortunei cuttings; Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 wild type and T3-generation 35S::CfAPX transgenic line L6; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for transient expression; AH109 yeast cells; Agrobacterium tumefaciens GV3101; E. coli DH5α.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Homology-based cloning and RACE to obtain full-length CfAPX cDNA from C. fortunei needles; sequence and phylogenetic analysis; bioinformatics prediction of physicochemical properties and secondary/tertiary structure; subcellular localization using 35S::CfAPX-GFP in N. benthamiana; yeast one-hybrid transcriptional activation and growth assays under NaCl and mannitol; generation of 35S::CfAPX Arabidopsis lines by floral dip; salt and drought stress treatments using NaCl, mannitol and PEG-6000; heat (40–42°C), cold (4°C) and freezing (-6°C) treatments; measurements of chlorophyll content, Fv/Fm, qP and YII, electrolyte leakage, APX/SOD/POD/CAT activities, H2O2, MDA and AsA contents; qRT-PCR to quantify CfAPX expression in different tissues and under stresses; statistical analysis by ANOVA.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous overexpression in yeast under NaCl and mannitol stress; stable overexpression in Arabidopsis thaliana (35S::CfAPX) followed by salt, drought, heat and freezing stress assays; qRT-PCR analysis of CfAPX stress-inducible expression in C. fortunei and transgenic Arabidopsis; physiological and biochemical measurements (antioxidant enzyme activities, AsA, H2O2, MDA, chlorophyll and chlorophyll fluorescence).
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate APX transcript (CL8464.Contig3_All) was identified from a C. fortunei RNA-seq database by BLAST using Pinus pinaster APX (PpAPX) as query; intermediate fragments were amplified from cDNA, and 5'/3' RACE was used to obtain full-length CfAPX cDNA from needles, followed by cloning, sequencing and assembly.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Overexpression of CfAPX enhances tolerance to salt, drought, heat and freezing by improving ROS scavenging and protecting photosystem II, indicating that CfAPX is a promising candidate gene for molecular breeding or genetic engineering of C. fortunei and other trees to improve multi-abiotic-stress tolerance and expand planting in harsh environments.
